title: atriplex canescens var. laciniata
intro: Atriplex canescens var. laciniata, also known as four-winged saltbush or laciniata four-wing saltbush, is a shrub-like plant native to North America, characterized by its four-winged seeds and silvery-gray foliage. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Atriplex canescens var. laciniata include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into teas, infusions, or salves, and can also be used in tinctures or as a topical application for skin issues.
